Minor: The draft long description says the series was decided 'four games to three' — OCR confirms this. No factual errors found in the core description.

The draft headline 'Richmond Sockeyes Win Abbott Cup, Edge Humboldt 5-4' is a reasonable summary but is AI-generated, not extracted from OCR. No clear standalone headline is visible in the OCR text — the article begins directly with 'S-P Services'.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat visiting Humboldt Broncos 5-4 in Game 7 to win the Abbott Cup Western Canada Tier Two junior hockey series four games to three.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes won the Abbott Cup best-of-seven Western Canada Tier Two junior hockey series four games to three over the Humboldt Broncos, with Dave Tomlinson scoring the series-clinching goal with 2:37 remaining in regulation time Wednesday night to snap a 4-4 tie. Richmond outscored Humboldt 26-24 across the series, with each team winning two one-goal games. Humboldt coach Bernie Lynch praised his team's effort but noted that a Centennial Cup win over Richmond would render the Abbott Cup result moot. The page also includes a companion article on the Broncos' strong financial preparations for hosting the Centennial Cup tournament, which officially opens in Humboldt on Friday.
review.notes: No standalone headline is visible in the OCR — the article opens directly with the byline 'S-P Services'. The AI-generated headline should be cleared or left blank. The page contains two articles: the Abbott Cup Game 7 recap (primary, Sockeyes-relevant) and a companion piece about Humboldt's Centennial Cup tournament finances. The long description has been updated to note the companion article. All core facts in the draft are accurate per the OCR.
